_comment: operation-router v2.4.4 config. Verified 2026-07-22 against codex-cli 0.144.5 models_cache: gpt-5.6-sol/terra/luna are exposed. The temporary test-environment Sol-to-Terra substitution has been removed and Operation 1 now uses gpt-5.6-sol as designed. Claude and Grok CLI capabilities remain as verified 2026-07-20: claude 2.1.212 (SKILL.md supports model/effort/disable-model-invocation/argument-hint; model IDs claude-opus-4-8/claude-sonnet-5/claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 recognized), grok 0.2.102 (only grok-4.5; grok --help confirms --always-approve, --allow, --deny; NO --no-auto-update flag/env/config exists). Do not hand-edit model IDs without re-verifying.

headlessPermissions._comment: v2.3.5 retains the v2.3.3 headless permission policy unchanged. grok --allow/--deny are compat aliases for Claude Code --allowedTools/--disallowedTools. mode=alwaysApprove maps to --always-approve: grok's built-in policy classifies heredoc/command-substitution commands as ask rules, and under the retired dontAsk mode a headless run cannot answer, so the whole session dies with cancellationCategory=PermissionCancelled (measured 2026-07-20, op2-issue3 debug log). --always-approve removes ask prompts while --deny rules still take priority and block matched commands as a graceful tool error (probe-verified). Trade-off: non-terminal tools are no longer constrained by the allow list; deny remains the effective guard.
headlessPermissions._deny_note: v2.4.0: first-line blocklist only. Pattern matching is brittle (flag reordering, wrappers, synonyms bypass it) and covers the Grok worker only. The uranium-backed guard is postflight repo-boundary detection (Test-RepoBoundaryViolation), which flags any change to watched out-of-repo sensitive files regardless of the command used.

1._effort_note: v2.4.0: medium→high. Operation 1 is high-risk and every other op1 slot (grok/sol/opus) is high; this was the sole outlier. Implementer↔reviewer separation kept (Opus end-reviews).

execution._comment: v2.4.4 worker host limits only the foreground wait. A timeout never starts a second worker; use recover with the same operation and issue.

quotaUnknownPatterns: you have exceeded your current quota
